examples.title: Array Reversal Using Pointers
examples.description: Reverses array elements using only pointer arithmetic
examples.explanation: Uses two pointers starting from both ends moving towards center, swapping elements. Demonstrates pointer initialization, comparison, dereferencing, and increment/decrement operations.

examples.title: String Length Using Pointers
examples.description: Finds string length without using strlen()
examples.explanation: Uses pointer to traverse string character by character. Each ++ moves pointer by 1 byte (sizeof(char)). Final method uses pointer subtraction to get count directly.

examples.title: Binary Search Using Pointers
examples.description: Binary search implemented with pointer arithmetic instead of indices
examples.explanation: Uses low and high pointers instead of indices. Pointer subtraction (high - low) gives number of elements. Mid calculation avoids overflow. Returns pointer or NULL. Demonstrates advanced pointer arithmetic.

examples.title: Pointer Arithmetic with Different Types
examples.description: Demonstrates how pointer arithmetic scales differently for various data types
examples.explanation: Shows that pointer increment moves by sizeof(type). char moves 1 byte, short moves 2, int/float 4, long/double 8 (typically). Demonstrates array access via pointer arithmetic.
